V-M-055.checks: Runtime emits and drains metadata-only telemetry through mtproto_proxy metrics, active-SNI sampling, and a private token-protected boundary without breaking MTProto traffic.
V-M-055.commands: pass-local 2026-05-19: node scripts/phase42-mtproto-telemetry-smoke.mjs && cd backend && SECRET_KEY=test-secret-key-with-enough-length PYTHONPATH=. ../.venv/bin/python -m pytest -o addopts='' tests/test_mtproto_runtime_telemetry_bridge.py -v; cd mtproto-runtime && rebar3 compile
V-M-055.evidence: Runtime telemetry snapshot/drain tests, live metric-backend/sampler static smoke, scheduler regression, and mtproto-runtime compile passed locally.

verification-levels.module-local: Use fake runtime events and private API fixtures to verify token checks, snapshot, drain, cursoring, overflow counters, redaction, and degraded runtime behavior.
verification-levels.wave-level: Run with V-M-054 to prove drained events become idempotent usage rows and with V-M-056 to prove stale runtime state is visible in analytics.
verification-levels.phase-level: Live release remains blocked until Phase-41 req_pq and Telegram Desktop/mobile proof standards pass after telemetry hooks are enabled.
verification-levels.phase-43-level: Runtime changes must prove trustworthy client IP source handling, source_ip_unavailable fallback, proxy CPU/RAM/uptime metrics, and unchanged Telegram proxy availability.

phase-43-checks.check-1: Client IP is emitted only when runtime can identify the real client endpoint, not RU/DE router internals.
phase-43-checks.check-2: When client IP is unavailable or untrusted, telemetry carries source_ip_unavailable and no guessed address.
phase-43-checks.check-3: Runtime resource metrics include proxy CPU percent, RAM bytes, uptime, dropped_events, and buffer status.
phase-43-checks.check-4: Resource metric collection failure degrades admin health only and does not restart or block the MTProto listener.

module-checks.check-1: Handshake, close, bytes, error, active_connection, unknown_sni, rejected_sni, and req_pq proof events use safe metadata fields only.
module-checks.check-2: Private telemetry endpoints reject missing or wrong runtime tokens and return no telemetry on unauthorized calls.
module-checks.check-3: Snapshot reads current counters without draining events.
module-checks.check-4: Drain returns cursor-ordered events and acknowledges only the returned batch.
module-checks.check-5: Bounded buffer overflow increments dropped_events and never blocks policy apply or MTProto listener behavior.
module-checks.check-6: Policy metadata maps known SNI to assignment_id and user_id while unknown SNI stays masked.
module-checks.check-7: Backend ingestion tolerates runtime outage and marks analytics stale or degraded only.
module-checks.check-8: mtproto_proxy metric_backend records bytes, protocol errors, protocol-ok, and connection counters into bounded sampler counters.
module-checks.check-9: Sampler records per-SNI active/handshake/close deltas from policy counters and avoids false per-user byte attribution when multiple domains are active.

scenarios.scenario-1: Handshake, close, bytes, error, active_connection, unknown_sni, rejected_sni, and req_pq proof events are represented with safe fields.
scenarios.scenario-2: Missing or wrong runtime token returns unauthorized and no telemetry leaks.
scenarios.scenario-3: Buffer overflow increments dropped_events but does not block policy apply or MTProto listener behavior.
scenarios.scenario-4: Backend ingestion tolerates runtime outage and marks analytics stale/degraded only.
scenarios.scenario-5: Telemetry hook changes do not alter the issued-link fake-TLS secret, SNI route, or Telegram client acceptance contract.
scenarios.scenario-6: Concurrent-domain traffic remains global_runtime unless a deeper data-plane hook can provide exact per-SNI bytes.
scenarios.scenario-7: Phase-43 client IP and resource metrics are emitted with safe source confidence and do not change issued-link fake-TLS behavior.

redaction-rules.rule-1: Runtime telemetry must exclude full tg links, fake-TLS secrets, base secret, salt, runtime token, private keys, and full promotion tags.
redaction-rules.rule-2: Runtime logs may include stable counters and masked SNI only.
redaction-rules.rule-3: Unauthorized telemetry responses must not reveal whether an assignment exists.

autonomous-execution-safety.safe-local: Runtime smoke may use local synthetic fixtures and fake private tokens.
autonomous-execution-safety.blocked-live: Changing live runtime telemetry hooks or restarting DE runtime requires operator approval and post-change req_pq proof.
